claims.claimText: Patellar Instability pathoanatomy centers on patella, extensor-mechanism, femoral-condyles and related stabilizers.

metadata.claim_text: Patellar Instability pathoanatomy centers on patella, extensor-mechanism, femoral-condyles and related stabilizers.

claims.claimText: Students often underweight exam findings and over-rely on imaging when evaluating patellar instability.

metadata.claim_text: Students often underweight exam findings and over-rely on imaging when evaluating patellar instability.

claims.claimText: State mechanism, key exam findings, imaging role, treatment concept, and return-to-play framework for patellar instability.

metadata.claim_text: State mechanism, key exam findings, imaging role, treatment concept, and return-to-play framework for patellar instability.

claims.claimText: Do not anchor on a single exam maneuver for patellar instability — integrate mechanism, instability symptoms, and imaging pattern.

metadata.claim_text: Do not anchor on a single exam maneuver for patellar instability — integrate mechanism, instability symptoms, and imaging pattern.

claims.claimText: Apprehension and J-sign suggest lateral patellar tracking and instability.

metadata.claim_text: Apprehension and J-sign suggest lateral patellar tracking and instability.

claims.claimText: Patellar instability is a tracking and soft-tissue restraint problem whose recurrence risk guides MPFL and bony procedure discussion.

claims.claimText: Rehabilitation milestones and return-to-play criteria for patellar instability require attending-defined progression gates.

decisionPoints.trigger: Active athlete with functional instability or mechanical symptoms from patellar instability despite appropriate initial management
decisionPoints.action: Discuss operative reconstruction versus continued rehabilitation; graft selection and fixation are attending decisions

decisionPoints.trigger: Athlete approaching return-to-sport after patellar instability treatment
decisionPoints.action: Apply sport-specific return-to-play criteria with strength, stability, and functional testing milestones
